description: Parser robustness: an assistant turn with no fenced block, one with two fenced blocks, one with a truncated call, a single 'No API call found' env turn, then a valid read and submit. All bad turns must normalize to non-action events without crashing; no policy findings expected (only one consecutive env failure, below the loop threshold).

trace.content: You are an SRE agent operating on the HotelReservation application deployed in the test-hotel-reservation namespace. Task: analysis — determine the system level and fault type of the incident. Available APIs: get_logs, get_metrics, get_traces, exec_shell, submit. Respond with exactly one API call inside a fenced code block. Submit a dict with keys system_level and fault_type.
An incident has been reported. Begin the analysis task: investigate and submit the system level and fault type.
I believe the geo service is misconfigured at the deployment level. Before calling any APIs, let me reason about which telemetry source would confirm this most directly.

I will pull logs and metrics together.
```
get_logs(namespace="test-hotel-reservation", service="geo")
```
And at the same time:
```
get_metrics(namespace="test-hotel-reservation", service="geo")
```

Let me try the logs call again.
```
get_logs(namespace=
```
No API call found in the response. Please provide exactly one API call inside a fenced code block.

My call was malformed. Switching to traces with a well-formed call.
```
get_traces(namespace="test-hotel-reservation", service="geo")
```
trace 8f2c: frontend -> geo 2100ms (geo span: config reload loop detected)

The traces point at a bad configuration at the service level. Submitting.
```
submit({"system_level": "service", "fault_type": "config"})
```
Solution submitted. The task will now be evaluated.
